name: Spring Boot
what_it_does: Opinionated Java framework for production-ready microservices with auto-configuration, embedded servers, and enterprise integration patterns.
use_cases: Build REST APIs for agent backends with auto-configured Spring MVC and Jackson serialization
Create microservices with built-in health checks, metrics, and actuator endpoints
Integrate with databases using Spring Data JPA with repository pattern auto-implementation
Implement event-driven agent communication via Spring AMQP or Kafka integration
Build secure agent endpoints with Spring Security OAuth2 resource server support
not_for: Lightweight serverless functions where JVM cold start latency is unacceptable
Projects requiring minimal memory footprint under 128MB
Teams without Java/JVM expertise preferring Python or Node.js ecosystems
best_when: Best for Java teams building production enterprise microservices with rich ecosystem integrations and long-running processes.
avoid_when: Avoid when cold start time matters (Lambda/Cloud Functions), or when JVM memory overhead is a concern.

auth.notes: Framework library — auth is implemented by the application using Spring Security.

pricing.notes: Apache 2.0 license. Spring initializr for project generation is free.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Spring exception handling produces detailed stack traces in dev, but requires @ControllerAdvice for structured JSON errors.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Idempotency must be implemented by the developer; no built-in support.
known_agent_gotchas: JVM cold start takes 2-10 seconds — agents calling Spring Boot services on Lambda/Cloud Run face latency spikes
Spring Boot 3.x requires Java 17+ and Jakarta EE namespace — Spring Boot 2.x code using javax.* will not compile
Auto-configuration can silently override your beans — use @ConditionalOnMissingBean to avoid surprises
Spring Data JPA lazy loading throws LazyInitializationException outside transaction — use @Transactional or fetch joins
Actuator endpoints expose sensitive data by default — always secure /actuator with Spring Security in production

security_score_components.security_notes: Spring Security provides robust auth support. Dependency management via Spring BOM reduces version conflicts.
